title: Severance Calculator Argentina 2026 — Art. 245 LCT | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Argentina Severance Pay Calculator (Dismissal Without Cause)
description: Calculate Argentine severance pay for unjust dismissal: Art. 245 seniority, notice period, month integration, proportional SAC, and unused vacation.
intro: Severance pay for unjust dismissal in Argentina is regulated by **Article 245 of the Labor Contract Law (LCT 20.744)** and consists of several components that add up: seniority indemnity (1 month per year), notice period pay, month integration, proportional SAC (aguinaldo), and unused vacation. This calculator gives you the **total gross final settlement** applying the Vizzoti ruling from the Supreme Court (floor of 67% of your best remuneration), the collective bargaining agreement cap if applicable, and the fraction-greater-than-3-months rule that adds an extra year to the computation. It works for employees under the LCT, any collective agreement (Commerce, Metalworkers, Construction, Healthcare, etc.), any seniority level, and takes into account that Art. 245 severance is tax-exempt per Supreme Court rulings.
keyTakeaway: **Seniority severance = best gross salary x computable years**. If the collective agreement cap reduces your base by more than 33%, the 67% of your salary is used (Vizzoti ruling). Add notice period + month integration + proportional SAC + unused vacation.
useCases: You were fired without cause and want to verify the amount owed before signing any agreement.
You're considering resigning but want to know what you'd receive if terminated instead.
You're a labor lawyer needing a quick calculation for a settlement negotiation.
HR offers a voluntary retirement package and you want to compare it with what the law mandates.
You have several years of seniority and want to estimate your severance before a salary increase.

fields.label: Best gross salary of the last year
fields.help: The best regular monthly gross remuneration of the last year worked (Art. 245 LCT).

fields.label: Additional months
fields.help: A fraction greater than 3 months adds one extra computable year for Art. 245.

fields.label: Day of the month of dismissal
fields.help: Used to calculate month integration (remaining days until end of month).

fields.label: Collective agreement cap (optional)
fields.help: 3x the average salary of the collective agreement. Leave at 0 if unknown (no cap).

explanation:
## How Severance for Unjust Dismissal Is Calculated

When an employer terminates an employee without cause in Argentina, they must pay a **final settlement** composed of several legally mandated components under the Labor Contract Law (LCT 20.744).

### 1. Seniority Indemnity (Art. 245 LCT)

The core component: **one month's salary per year worked** (or fraction greater than 3 months = one additional year). The base is the **best regular monthly gross remuneration of the last year**.

```
Art. 245 Indemnity = best_salary x computable_years
```

**Example:** 4 years and 5 months = 5 computable years (because 5 > 3). 4 years and 2 months = 4 computable years.

### 2. Collective Agreement Cap and Vizzoti Ruling

The original Art. 245 applies a cap of **3x the average salary of the collective bargaining agreement**. However, the **Vizzoti v. AMSA** ruling by the Supreme Court (09/14/2004) declared it unconstitutional when the cap reduces the base by more than **33%**. Therefore, **67% of your best remuneration** is used as a floor.

| Situation | Base Applied |
|---|---|
| Your salary <= agreement cap | Your full salary |
| Your salary > cap but cap > 67% of salary | Agreement cap |
| Your salary > cap and cap < 67% of salary | **67% of your salary** (Vizzoti) |

### 3. Notice Period (Arts. 231-232 LCT)

If the employer doesn't give advance notice, they must pay:

| Seniority | Notice Period |
|---|---|
| Less than 3 months | 15 days |
| 3 months - 5 years | 1 month |
| More than 5 years | 2 months |

Notice period pay includes **SAC on notice** (1/12 of the amount).

### 4. Month Integration (Art. 233 LCT)

You're entitled to pay for the remaining days of the month (from dismissal date to month end), plus SAC on that amount.

### 5. Proportional SAC

Proportional aguinaldo for the current semester: `(salary / 2) x (months worked in semester / 6)`.

### 6. Unused Vacation + SAC

Days earned in the current year, paid at **salary / 25** per day (vacation daily rate, Art. 155 LCT). SAC on vacation is added (1/12).

### Vacation Days by Seniority

| Seniority | Vacation Days |
|---|---|
| Up to 5 years | 14 calendar days |
| 5 - 10 years | 21 calendar days |
| 10 - 20 years | 28 calendar days |
| More than 20 years | 35 calendar days |

## Tax Treatment

- **Art. 245 (seniority)**: EXEMPT from income tax (Supreme Court rulings "Negri" and "De Lorenzo"). No social security contributions.
- **Notice period**: subject to income tax and social security if above the tax-free threshold.
- **Month integration**: subject to income tax and social security like regular salary.
- **Proportional SAC and vacation**: subject to income tax and social security.

## What This Calculator Does NOT Include

- **Moral damages or discriminatory dismissal**: Arts. 178, 182 (pregnancy, marriage) multiply Art. 245 by 13.
- **Art. 80 penalty**: if the employer doesn't provide the employment certificate (3 additional salaries).
- **Law 25.323 Art. 1**: unregistered employment (doubles the indemnity).
- **Law 25.323 Art. 2**: if you must go to court to collect (50% surcharge).
- **Labor lawsuit costs**: legal fees and court costs calculated separately.

## Common Mistakes

1. **Using net salary**: Art. 245 is always based on GROSS best remuneration.
2. **Not counting the fraction over 3 months**: 4 years and 4 months = 5 years, not 4.
3. **Applying the agreement cap without checking Vizzoti**: Vizzoti almost always gives a higher number.
4. **Forgetting SAC on notice, integration, and vacation**: these are separate line items in the settlement.
5. **Not requesting the employment certificate**: if not provided, you're owed 3 additional salaries (Art. 80).

faq.q: What components are included in a final settlement for unjust dismissal?
faq.a: Six components: (1) **seniority indemnity** (Art. 245, 1 month per year), (2) **notice period** (15 days, 1 month, or 2 months depending on seniority), (3) **month integration** (remaining days of the dismissal month), (4) **proportional SAC (aguinaldo)**, (5) **unused vacation**, and (6) **SAC on notice, integration, and vacation**. The calculator adds them all automatically.

faq.q: What happens if I have 4 years and 4 months of seniority?
faq.a: That counts as **5 computable years**. The 4-month fraction exceeds 3 months, so it rounds up to a full additional year for Art. 245 purposes. If you had 4 years and 2 months, it would be only 4 computable years.

faq.q: What is the Vizzoti ruling and when does it apply?
faq.a: A Supreme Court ruling (09/14/2004) that declared it unconstitutional to apply the collective agreement cap when it reduces the severance base by more than **33%**. In practice, **67% of your best remuneration** is almost always used as the floor, because agreement caps tend to be low relative to actual salaries.

faq.q: Is the severance payment subject to income tax?
faq.a: The **Art. 245 seniority indemnity** is **tax-exempt** per Supreme Court rulings "Negri" (2014) and "De Lorenzo" (2009). It's also exempt from social security contributions (17%). However, **notice period, month integration, proportional SAC, and vacation pay are taxable** just like regular salary.

faq.q: What if the employer doesn't pay the full amount?
faq.a: You can file a claim under **Law 25.323 Art. 2**, which increases the severance by up to **50%** if you must go to court to collect. Before litigation, you must attempt mediation (mandatory in Buenos Aires). Consult a labor lawyer.

faq.q: Is seniority counted from actual start date or registration date?
faq.a: **From the actual start date**, always. If you worked informally ('off the books') for a period, you can claim it under **Law 24.013** (National Employment Law), which also **doubles the severance** if the employment was not properly registered. You need to prove it with witnesses, receipts, messages, or bank records.

faq.q: How much notice period pay am I entitled to?
faq.a: Depends on seniority: **15 days** if under 3 months, **1 month** between 3 months and 5 years, **2 months** if over 5 years. The employer pays the equivalent salary for that period plus proportional SAC (1/12) on the amount.

Using the formula **salary / 25 x corresponding days** (vacation daily rate, Art. 155 LCT). Days depend on seniority: 14 days up to 5 years, 21 days from 5 to 10, 28 days from 10 to 20, and 35 days with 20+ years. It's prorated based on months worked in the current year.

sources.name: Supreme Court Ruling: Vizzoti v. AMSA (09/14/2004)

example.title: Example: Dismissal with 7 years 5 months and ARS 2,000,000 gross
steps: **Best gross salary** of the last year: ARS 2,000,000.
**Seniority**: 7 years and 5 months = **8 computable years** (5-month fraction > 3 months adds 1 year).
**Month integration** (dismissed on day 10, 20 days remaining): `2,000,000 x 20/30` = ARS 1,333,333. + **SAC on integration** = ARS 111,111.
**Proportional SAC** (4 months in semester): `2,000,000 / 2 x 4/6` = ARS 666,667.
**Unused vacation** (5 months = 6 days): `2,000,000 / 25 x 6` = ARS 480,000. + SAC on vacation = ARS 40,000.
example.result: **Total gross**: approx. **ARS 22,964,444**. Notice period and month integration are subject to 17% social security deductions and potentially income tax. The Art. 245 seniority indemnity is tax-exempt.
